intro: Rondeletia hispidula, also known as Rough Rondeletia, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Rondeletia hispidula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rondeletia hispidula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever, arthritis, and gastrointestinal disorders.
